summary: No new third-party dependency is required for this phase. Three of the four assessment blockers are already solved by existing first-party assets: UAR's published container image, librefang's own sidecar supervisor (spawn + restart + backoff), and librefang's existing reqwest/futures/tokio-stream HTTP+SSE stack. The remaining work is small, confined, in-tree code plus one additive upstream change to UAR's release pipeline.

candidates.need: Ship a UAR binary so it can be located and executed at runtime
candidates.mechanism: multi-stage `COPY --from=<uar-image>` into librefang's Dockerfile; bundle beside `librefang` in the release tarball
candidates.evidence: UAR .github/workflows/build-image.yml:19,69-71 (image built + pushed); UAR Dockerfile:264-267 (binary + /opt/uar/{static,skills,models}); Dockerfile:281 CMD; healthcheck port 1906 (Dockerfile:279)
rejected_alternatives: Build UAR from source inside librefang's Dockerfile — reintroduces UAR's build.rs (network models.dev fetch + pnpm frontend) into librefang's hot build path; this is the cost we are trying to shed

candidates.need: Spawn, health-check, restart-with-backoff, and gracefully shut down a child process
candidates.mechanism: Extend the existing supervisor; add the READY:{port} stdout handshake and stdin-EOF shutdown, which it does not currently have
candidates.evidence: sidecar.rs:502-520 already exposes restart, initial_backoff_ms, max_backoff_ms, max_retries, reset_after_secs; sidecar.rs:777-827 spawn_once + stderr classification
rejected_alternatives: External Rust supervision crate — would duplicate working, proven in-tree code and add a dependency for no benefit

candidates.need: Locate the UAR binary deterministically; never depend on PATH
candidates.choice: Generalize resolve_sidecar_command from a Telegram-only special case to a stem-parameterized helper
candidates.mechanism: current_exe() dir -> <home_dir>/bin/ -> PATH fallback (algorithm unchanged, stem becomes a parameter)
candidates.evidence: sidecar.rs:728 resolve_sidecar_command; TELEGRAM_SIDECAR_STEM at :699; call site SidecarAdapter::new sidecar.rs:1468. Today any non-Telegram command (including universal-agent-runtime) falls through to a bare OS PATH lookup — this is the reported bug.
rejected_alternatives: Copy-paste a second UAR-specific resolver — divergence risk; generalizing the proven one is strictly safer
which/which-rs crate — solves PATH lookup, which is exactly the mechanism we are trying to NOT depend on

candidates.need: UarDriver becomes an HTTP + SSE client instead of an in-process library call
candidates.choice: reqwest 0.13 (stream feature) + futures + tokio-stream, already in the workspace
candidates.mechanism: POST /api/chat/completion; consume SSE from /api/live; health via /healthz + /readyz
candidates.evidence: workspace Cargo.toml:136 reqwest 0.13 with "stream"; :47 tokio-stream; :156 futures. SSE already consumed in-tree (librefang-runtime-mcp). UAR routes at src/server.rs:694+
rejected_alternatives: reqwest-eventsource / eventsource-client — an added dependency for a stream we can already consume with reqwest's stream feature
tonic/gRPC — UAR exposes it, but HTTP+SSE covers completions and token streaming at far lower integration cost

candidates.need: Config surface for a UAR sidecar
candidates.mechanism: Add enabled, binary stem/command, endpoint override, and restart/backoff knobs; mirror SidecarChannelConfig field names for one consistent operator vocabulary
candidates.evidence: UarConfig today has only api_key/model/surreal_data_dir/base_url/remote/share_librefang_storage — no binary path, no enable flag, no endpoint-to-spawn. base_url is an LLM-provider override, NOT a UAR endpoint (drivers/uar.rs:97).

candidates.need: Web console can run / stop / restart / test UAR
candidates.choice: Dashboard query + mutation hooks (per CLAUDE.md data-layer rules)
candidates.mechanism: status pill <- supervisor state + /healthz,/readyz; start/stop/restart <- supervisor commands; 'Test' button <- POST /api/chat/completion; model picker <- GET /api/models
candidates.evidence: All backing endpoints already exist (UAR src/server.rs:694+). Today the dashboard's only UAR affordances are manifest import (POST /api/agents/uar) and SurrealDB namespace linking (POST /api/storage/link-uar) — neither touches a process.

candidates.need: Remove the surrealdb lockstep pin and UAR's build cost from the default build
candidates.choice: Drop the unconditional `features = ["uar-driver"]` edge at librefang-kernel/Cargo.toml:16
candidates.mechanism: One-line Cargo.toml change; makes the feature genuinely opt-in
candidates.evidence: cargo tree -i surrealdb: universal-agent-runtime pins =3.2.1 (EXACT, rigid); surreal-memory pins 3.2.0 (CARET, flexible). UAR is the SOLE source of the lockstep constraint.

upstream_changes_required.change: Build and publish the `uar-sidecar` binary (currently only `universal-agent-runtime` is built)
upstream_changes_required.evidence: grep -c uar-sidecar .github/workflows/release.yml => 0; Dockerfile:225 builds only `--bin universal-agent-runtime`; release matrix ships only universal-agent-runtime-{linux-x64,macos-x64,macos-arm64,windows-x64.exe}
upstream_changes_required.note: Same repo and branch family we already cut this session (sync-gqadonis-8c7377a1).

risks.risk: UAR's cross-platform prebuilt binaries are self-described as 'an aspirational extra' (release.yml:160) and may not build reliably
risks.impact: Blocks the desktop/local shape only. Linux/container (the reported bug) is unaffected because the binary comes from the image.
risks.mitigation: Verify the macOS/Windows release targets before promising local support; scope v1 to Linux/container if they are unreliable.

risks.risk: Baking UAR's binary + /opt/uar/{static,skills,models} into the BossFang image grows it
risks.mitigation: Quantify before committing; determine which asset dirs uar-sidecar actually needs at runtime; consider first-run fetch or a shared volume if the models dir is large.

risks.risk: With the cargo-level pin gone, librefang's HTTP client and UAR's API can drift independently
risks.impact: Silent incompatibility at runtime rather than a compile error
risks.mitigation: Pin the UAR image tag; add a startup version/capability check.
